senses.definition: a chemical compound containing two chlorine atoms bonded to another element or group
examples: potassium bichloride is used in certain chemical reactions.
the lab prepared a solution of copper bichloride for the experiment.

senses.definition: plural form of bichloride, referring to more than one bichloride compound
examples: the sample contained several different bichlorides.
researchers tested several bichlorides to compare reactivity.

senses.definition: a musical term for two simultaneous notes or tones; a pair of chords sounding together
examples: the duo played a bichord harmony to accompany the melody.
a bichord texture emerged in the early Baroque composition.

senses.definition: a term in some languages meaning bugs or insects; used in English mainly as a loanword or in proper names
examples: the field was full of bichos buzzing around.
a restaurant named Bichos serves Mexican dishes.

senses.definition: a chemical compound containing two chromate groups; often used in chemistry and dye processes
examples: bichromate salts are used as oxidizing agents in certain reactions.
the solution contained bichromate before it was neutralized.

senses.definition: containing or coated with bichromate; chemically treated with bichromate
examples: bichromated film requires special handling.
the paper was bichromated to create a sensitive layer.

senses.definition: plural of bichromate; salts or esters derived from two chromate groups
examples: these pigments are formed from various bichromates.
solutions of bichromates are used in photographic processes.

senses.definition: involving two colors or two colours; having two colors
examples: the flag has a bichromatic design in blue and white.
a bichromatic image uses two contrasting colors.

senses.definition: to color or stain with two colors; to treat with bichromate in a chemical process
examples: the photographer attempted to bichromatize the print for effect.
they bichromatized the material to enhance contrast.

senses.definition: a two-colored artistic or printed work or page; a coloring in two colors
examples: the illustration was done in black and red bichrome.
a bichrome print can look striking.

senses.definition: composed of two colors
examples: the bichrome poster uses blue and gold.
a bichrome map highlights two regions.

senses.definition: relating to two colors; two-colored
examples: a bichromic light spectrum can aid certain imaging techniques.

senses.definition: a colloquial or informal term for a person from Brittany in France; also a surname or place name in some contexts
examples: the bichy from the coastal town shared stories.
bichy foods are popular in the region.

senses.definition: having two cilia or hair-like projections
examples: the cell is biciliate and has two small cilia.
a biciliate organism can move differently from uniciliate types.

senses.definition: having two cilia; equipped with two cilia
examples: a biciliated protozoan was observed under the microscope.

senses.definition: relating to or formed by two heads, especially a muscle with two heads
examples: the bicipital muscle anchors the upper arm.
a bicipital groove on the humerus helps muscle attachment.

senses.definition: having two heads; double-headed; two-headed
examples: a bicipitous structure supports both sides.
the bicipitous appendage resembles a forked branch.

senses.definition: having two circular paths or shapes; involving two circles
examples: the two rings form a bicircular design.
a bicircular route passes around both lakes.

senses.definition: having two rose-shaped structures or petals; two-rose form
examples: the flower shows a bicirrose pattern.

senses.definition: to argue or quarrel in a petty or persistent way
examples: they would bick all day about trivial matters.
the siblings bicked over who sat in the front seat.

senses.definition: to argue or quarrel about small or unimportant matters
examples: the colleagues bickered over the wording of the memo.
they bickered briefly but soon forgave each other.

senses.definition: past tense of bicker; argued or quarreled over small matters
examples: they bickered about the bill last night.
the two teams bickered but settled the dispute.

senses.definition: a person who bickers; someone who argues over small matters
examples: the bickerer in the group often raised petty complaints.
three bickerers argued in the hallway.

senses.definition: plural of bickerer; multiple people who bicker
examples: the corner was filled with bickerers during the meeting.

senses.definition: the act of arguing about small, unimportant matters
examples: their constant bickering was tiring.
the meeting ended after a long period of bickering.

senses.definition: present participle of bicker; arguing about small matters
examples: they were bickering about nothing all afternoon.

senses.definition: plural of bickering; instances of petty arguing
examples: the report noted several bickerings between departments.
two or three bickerings occurred during the workshop.
